chunks.title: Introduction to Frailty and Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker emphasizes that frailty is a leading cause of death and advocates for a diet rich in meat and organs combined with carbohydrates to avoid it. The podcast aims to debunk arguments that suggest animal-based diets accelerate aging.
chunks.transcript: Frailty is the number one cause of your dying and you will avoid Frailty by eating meat and organs along with good sources of carbohydrates on this week's podcast the debunking had to come out someone sent me an article and it's a long article so I hit the highlights in this podcast about why meat accelerates aging and why meat is bad for you from all the angles so I thought it was kind of a comprehensive article that I would attempt to debunk as best as possible to provide more evidence for that for you all I've done multiple podcasts with vegans in the past and this is more evidence in this podcast more things for you to chew on about why meat is not harmful for humans but the meat and organs desiccated like hard and soil or fresh are essential for every human who hopes to be optimal so enjoy this

chunks.title: Debunking Criticism of Meat
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses various arguments found in a recently published article that claims high animal protein diets lead to accelerated aging. They plan to address and refute these points, emphasizing that previous studies have shortcomings.
chunks.transcript: podcast essentially a takedown of most of the vegan plant-based arguments about why meat is bad for your health on to the podcast I get asked frequently to do more debates with plant-based Advocates and in the show notes I will put a link for previous debates I've done with plant-based Advocates vegans Etc I've done multiple episodes with this on the podcast including plant-based Advocates like Joel Furman and many others but these are difficult to do for a variety of reasons it's difficult to get vegans and plant-based Advocates to actually come on the podcast it's difficult to get them to agree to a debate in the first place and it's difficult to find platforms when they won't come on my show that they're happy with so in lieu of more of those plant-based debates though I hope more will happen in the future this week I will tackle head on a pretty crazy article that was sent to me by someone in email talking about why animal protein Rich diets accelerate aging and wrapped into this article is a lot of conjecture there's a lot of epidemiology and observational evidence and a lot of I would say false paradigms false thinking around all of this that creates a total House of Cards I've done a podcast on longevity in the past please refer to that one if you would like lots of detail here having said that this podcast will be very detailed there will be a lot of articles in this podcast and it will be complex so I'll do my best throughout it to stay high level but there's no really high level way to do this but I thought this article

chunks.title: Healthy User Bias and Confounding in Studies
chunks.summary: The concept of healthy user bias is introduced, discussing how individuals on plant-based diets may also engage in other healthy behaviors. The speaker points out the limitations of observational studies that support anti-meat narratives.
chunks.transcript: they will in this article as I will show you repeatedly consistently point to epidemiology observational studies to remind us that in the Western World plant-based dieters sometimes appear to be better from a cardiovascular disease perspective or a morbidity and mortality perspective now again these are observational studies there is no actual research here with an intervention these are surveys that are done and we know these are confounded consistently by things called healthy user bias and unhealthy user bias if you're not familiar with those terms healthy user bias means that people who generally eat plant-based do other healthy behaviors because what has been the narrative in the West for the last 70 years that meat is bad for you that's thanks to Ansel keys in the 1950s and 1960s and unhealthy user bias is the exact opposite we're looking at people who still eat meat in spite of the dietary advice which is prevailing and they also tend to drink more smoke more not go to colonoscopies mammograms they don't play tennis on Sunday with their friends they're also a lower socioeconomic status they may or may not ride motorcycles they may do dangerous things you get the idea unhealthy user bias Health user bias and we know this to be the case confounding so many any of these studies looking at meat and I'll show evidence for that later in this podcast and I'll show evidence that if you look in Asia where the narrative is completely different specifically that meat is associated with affluence men who eat the most red meat have the lowest rates of heart
